mnemonics: SAD RAD FURRY CREW (for Pressman's process flow: Specification -> Analysis & Design -> Fabrication -> Testing -> Deployment -> Review -> Retirement)
EVIL CROWD (for McCabe's Essential Paths: Edge, Vertex, Independent Paths, Loops + Complexity, Regions, Outdegree, Weights, Decision-to-decision graphs)
PROUD QA (for quality attributes: Performance, Reliability, Operability, Usability, Dependability)
keyPoints: Software engineering is the application of a systematic, disciplined, quantifiable approach to the development, operation, and maintenance of software (the 'engineering' aspect).
The Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) provides a framework of processes for developing software, with models like Waterfall, Incremental, and Agile.
Software quality is defined by its conformance to explicit functional and performance requirements and implicit expectations (SRS document).
commonMistakes: Treating the chosen process model (e.g., Waterfall) as a rigid, sequential checklist rather than an adaptable framework.
Focusing solely on unit testing and neglecting higher-level testing like integration, system, and acceptance testing.
Equating 'working software' with 'quality software', ignoring non-functional requirements like maintainability and security.
